paragraphs.body: When I see your smile I know I did something good; I can see it in the way your eyes light up the way your whole face softens and in that moment I know despite all my fears despite all my doubts I’ve done something right it’s the kind of smile that tells me everything is going to be okay even when things feel uncertain you know when we would walk on the treadmill together I always remember us playing Miley Cyrus and I think of how those little moments meant so much to me even when you didn’t realize it every time we would go to Justice and shop together you always picked out the clothes that I’d want to wear even when I didn’t know myself when I get in your car and I smell your perfume I know you’ve been driving it; I can tell it’s a simple thing but it reminds me of you and your care and how you always leave little pieces of yourself with me I want you to understand how much you’ve shaped me in ways you may never even know one of my biggest fears is losing you and not being able to show you everything I’ve worked for everything I’ve become; I know you always say I’m capable and you’ve always believed in me but there are days when I can’t see it I doubt myself and the things you’ve told me I remember when I was little how you always told me I was brave and intelligent that I was going to be successful; you used to say I was going to do amazing things and on those days when I don’t believe it when I feel like I’m failing I remember those words and they carry me through you have taught me to push through the doubts and keep moving forward even when I feel like I can’t I remember when I first told you I wanted to be a nurse just like you and you smiled and told me how proud you were; but I didn’t stop there I wanted to push myself further to challenge myself so I told you I wanted to be a physician assistant and I saw your eyes light up I knew then that I had your support no matter how hard it gets no matter how much I doubt myself I know I can count on you I remember when we used to go to the park after preschool and walk around together with my best friend; I remember how we would always go see the Christmas lights together every year and how that tradition became something special I remember how when we got the pool put in we spent all that time mulching and putting down rocks working together on something that became a part of our lives I think about those moments often because they remind me of how we shared small moments together and how those moments became memories that I hold onto every time I see someone putting in a pool I think about how we worked so hard to make that place special for our family and when I tell you I didn’t do well on an exam I remember the look you give me; I see your concern I see the way you want me to do better because you know I can you always push me to be the best version of myself even when I’m not sure I can do it and when someone says they hate driving I always think about you because you taught me how to be patient behind the wheel how to enjoy the journey instead of rushing to the destination when I go out to eat steak it reminds me of you because I know it’s your favorite; it’s the little things that we share that make me feel closer to you even when we’re far apart I know you worry about me sometimes you tell me to take time for myself to make sure I’m not pushing too hard but I can’t help it; I want to make you proud I want to show you that I’ve become the person you always believed I could be I remember how you warned me about the challenges of being a physician assistant and how hard it would be but I also know that I’m ready for it I’m ready to work through the tough times because I know I have you behind me cheering me on so when I walk across that stage with my degree in my hand I want you to know that I did it; I did it for you I did it because of you because of all the love and support you’ve given me over the years I want you to see everything I promised to do come to life and know that you played the biggest part in it I couldn’t have done it without you your words your love your belief in me have shaped who I am and what I strive for every day thank you for being my role model for always teaching me how to handle the hard times and for being the person who always believed in me when I couldn’t believe in myself I want you to see me succeed to see that everything you taught me is reflected in who I’ve become because no matter what happens in my life I’ll always carry your lessons with me and I’ll always make sure you know how much I appreciate everything you’ve done for me you’ve shaped my life in ways you may never understand but I know I wouldn’t be who I am without you and when I see your smile I’ll know I did something good; why do you think I always prioritize making you happy? what do you think I will enjoy the most about being a PA? what is your favorite memory of me when I was younger? why do you think we have such similar personality traits? how did you cope with your struggles through nursing school?

annotations.text: I remember when I first told you I wanted to be a nurse just like you and you smiled and told me how proud you were; but I didn’t stop there I wanted to push myself further to challenge myself so I told you I wanted to be a physician assistant and I saw your eyes light up
annotations.comment: This passage really stands out to me. You're including some really great details such as mentioning you decided to be a nurse and that your mom is a nurse--this really strengthens the reader's understanding of the relationship between the two of you.

annotations.text: it’s the little things that we share that make me feel closer to you even when we’re far apart I know you worry about me sometimes you tell me to take time for myself to make sure I’m not pushing too hard but I can’t help it;
annotations.comment: There are occasional passages like this that I would love to see you explore in greater detail. Could you describe what you mean by little things here? When are you likely to push yourself too hard? What do you mean you can't help it? What does she suggest you should do when you take time for yourself?

Hello, The central focusing idea of this piece is clearly a description of the development of your relationship with your mom. You do a great job of focusing on that throughout the piece and not veering off topic or convoluting it with red herrings. Meanwhile, this central idea is supported by the big ideas such as experiences you’ve had together, items you share between you, and, of course, conversations you’ve had together. The words that stood out the most to me are things like success, support, good, great, happy—words that describe how you have overcome and will overcome challenges in your own life, and how closely connected all those words are to your mother. These words really reinforce the overall positivity of the essay. If you’ve had any bad moments or trying experiences or difficult conversations with your mom, your reader doesn’t feel any of that negativity at all. The thing I admire most about this piece, in fact, is that overwhelming positivity. If there is doubt in your life, there is none about your relationship with your mom. If you have fears, it is not that your mom doesn’t love you. If you are worried or tired or stressed, your mom is there to carry those burdens for you. It’s really nice to see this relationship taking place on paper. On the surface of things, the central audience of this piece is clearly your mom. You have written a celebration of your relationship in the second person to her. Beyond that the audience of the piece is anybody who wants to celebrate their own relationship with someone else. You have done a great job of showing an audience how to be joyful in expressing your love for someone else without being overly sentimental or cheesy. What I’ve learned from this is about how much of your mother you see in yourself. Clearly, you treasure your experiences with her, but you also cherish them all as memories. She is a large part of your mind, and she always will be. It was a pleasure reading this very personal essay. Thanks,
